document.title: IEC 60601-1 Medical electrical equipment Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance
document.documentType: CB Test Report and CB Test Certificate excerpt
document.revisionOrVersion: Report Reference No. 269183; TRF No. IEC60601_1J; Master TRF 2014-07

sourceSummary.oneLine: Nemko Korea CB Scheme IEC 60601-1 test report No. 269183 covers DEPS9601, a Class I component power supply intended for medical electrical end-product use.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports CB safety certification and end-product regulatory review by documenting IEC 60601-1 test scope, classifications, ratings, national differences, exclusions, and deferred end-product evaluations.
keyPoints: The tested item is a DEPS9601 power supply with input 1.0 A, 100-240 Vac, 50/60 Hz and output 3 A, 16 Vdc.
The report was issued by Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. on 2014-09-18 under CB Scheme procedure.
The equipment was evaluated as Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment, and not intended for patient connection.
Risk management files according to ISO 14971:2007 were not provided or evaluated; risk management is deferred to the end product.
Primary-to-secondary insulation was evaluated as 2MOOP; MOPP use requires additional testing based on end use.
End-product evaluations are required for leakage current, power cord set, biocompatibility, EMC, applied parts, SIP/SOP, and several marking/documentation items.

entities.name: DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.
entities.evidence: Applicant, manufacturer, and factory are listed as DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD., RM 903 Ace Gwangmyeong Tower, Soha-dong, Gwangmyeong-si, Gyeonggi-do, Korea.

entities.name: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: CB Testing Laboratory is Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. at 153, Osan-Ro, Mohyeon-Myeon, Cheoin-Gu, Yongin-Si, Gyeonggi-Do, Korea.

entities.name: Nemko
entities.evidence: The report states it is issued under Nemko responsibility and is valid when bearing the Nemko name and logo.

entities.name: DEPS9601
entities.evidence: Model/Type reference for the test item description power supply is DEPS9601.

entities.name: SDR-G4335W
entities.evidence: The power supply is intended for connection to the X-Ray detector, type SDR-G4335W, manufactured by Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.

entities.name: Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: The X-Ray detector SDR-G4335W is identified as manufactured by Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.

entities.name: IECEE
entities.evidence: The report uses the IECEE CB Scheme procedure and states it is not valid as a CB Test Report unless appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB.

concepts.name: Class I component power supply
concepts.evidence: General product information states the equipment under testing is a class I power supply with metal enclosure and continuous operation.

concepts.name: 2MOOP primary-secondary insulation
concepts.evidence: The insulation was evaluated to comply with 2MOOP insulation between primary and secondary circuit.

concepts.name: Risk management deferred to end product
concepts.evidence: Risk management files according to ISO 14971:2007 were not provided and not evaluated; risk management shall be considered in the end product.

concepts.name: No patient applied part evaluation
concepts.evidence: The equipment has no patient applied part and shall be evaluated in the end product application.

concepts.name: End-product EMC evaluation
concepts.evidence: The end-use equipment shall be evaluated for compliance with latest EMC requirements according to IEC 60601-1-2.

concepts.name: National differences
concepts.evidence: National differences are listed for CENELEC members and CB members Canada, Japan, Korea, Switzerland, and United States.

evidence.claim: CB Test Report No. 269183 was issued for IEC 60601-1 medical electrical equipment safety requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Report Reference No.: 269183; Date of issue: 2014-09-18; IEC 60601-1 Medical electrical equipment Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance.

evidence.claim: The test procedure was the IECEE CB Scheme.
evidence.evidenceText: Test procedure: CB Scheme; the report states it is not valid as a CB Test Report unless appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB in accordance with IECEE 02.

evidence.claim: The tested product is a DEPS9601 power supply.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item description: Power supply; Model/Type reference: DEPS9601.

evidence.claim: The DEPS9601 ratings are input 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Class I, and output 3 A at 16 Vdc.
evidence.evidenceText: Ratings: Input: 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Cl. I; Output: 3 A, 16 Vdc.

evidence.claim: Testing was performed by Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: CB Testing Laboratory: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.; testing location/address: 153, Osan-Ro, Mohyeon-Myeon, Cheoin-Gu, Yongin-Si, Gyeonggi-Do, Korea.

evidence.claim: The report includes attachments for equipment pictures, national deviations, PCB layout and circuit diagram, and switching transformer specification.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachments listed: Pictures of equipment, National deviations, PCB layout and circuit diagram, and Specification of switching transformer (T1).

evidence.claim: The tested equipment is intended as a non-patient-environment component power supply, with installation suitability checked in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is intended to be used in non-patient environment as a component (power supply). The suitability of installation shall be checked in the end product.

evidence.claim: Risk management according to ISO 14971:2007 was not evaluated for the component power supply.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk management files according to ISO 14971:2007 were not provided, and not evaluated in this test report.

evidence.claim: The equipment was evaluated for safety at 40 °C despite a manufacturer-recommended operating ambient of 35 °C.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer recommended operating ambient is 35 °C. However, the equipment has been evaluated for safety at 40 °C and complies with this standard.

evidence.claim: The equipment was evaluated as Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment, not for flammable anesthetic mixtures, and not intended for patient connection.
evidence.evidenceText: Equipment has been evaluated as Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment and has not been evaluated for use in the presence of flammable anaesthetic mixture; not intended for patient connection.

evidence.claim: Primary-to-secondary insulation was evaluated as 2MOOP, while MOPP use requires additional testing.
evidence.evidenceText: The insulation has been evaluated to comply with 2MOOP insulation between primary and secondary circuit; when MOPP is needed, additional testing shall be conducted.

evidence.claim: The power supply cord set was not evaluated and must be approved according to national regulations in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: The end product shall be provided with an approved mains cord set complying with national regulations; the power supply cord set was not evaluated in this report.

evidence.claim: Biocompatibility compliance must be determined at end-use equipment level under ISO 10993-1.
evidence.evidenceText: The end-use equipment must be evaluated to determine compliance with the requirements of ISO 10993-1.

evidence.claim: The end-use equipment must be evaluated for EMC compliance under IEC 60601-1-2.
evidence.evidenceText: The end-use equipment shall be evaluated to determine compliance with the latest EMC requirements according to IEC 60601-1-2.

evidence.claim: The product fulfilled IEC 60601-1 and EN 60601-1 requirements listed in the summary of national differences.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of IEC 60601-1:2005 + CORR.1 + CORR.2 + A1:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A11:2011 + A1:2013.

evidence.claim: The sample was received on 2014-07-18 and tests were performed from 2014-07-21 to 2014-09-16.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of receipt of test item(s): 2014-07-18; Dates tests performed: 2014-07-21 to 2014-09-16.

evidence.claim: The DEPS9601 is intended for connection to Samsung X-Ray detector type SDR-G4335W.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment is intended for connection to the X-Ray detector, type SDR-G4335W manufactured by Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The report defers several IEC 60601-1 clauses and risk management topics to the end-product investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: Scope of Power Supply evaluation defers clauses 7.5, 7.9, 9 except 9.1 and 9.3, 10, 14, 16; Risk Management was excluded.

evidence.claim: Fault testing between 24 Vdc and 16 Vdc showed no hazard between the secondary circuits, but SIP/SOP and patient connection still require end-product evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Fault testing was conducted between 24 Vdc and 16 Vdc to show no hazard; compliance of SIP/SOP and output circuits for direct patient connection still require end-product evaluation.

evidence.claim: Certified components are used according to their ratings and certifications.
evidence.evidenceText: Certified components are used in accordance with their ratings and certifications, and they comply with applicable parts of this standard.

evidence.claim: Humidity preconditioning was performed at 32 °C and 93% RH for 48 hours.
evidence.evidenceText: Relevant tests subjected to humidity preconditioning; performed at 32 °C, 93% R.H. for 48 h.

evidence.claim: Markings include supply voltage range, frequency, input current, output voltage/current, and fuse rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Marked supply range 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, input 1.0 A, outputs DC +16 V 3 A, and fuse rating T6.3AH 250V.

evidence.claim: The protective earth terminal is provided as part of the appliance inlet and marked internally.
evidence.evidenceText: Protective earth terminal provided as part of appliance inlet according to IEC 60320; symbol 6 in Table D.1 is marked near internal terminal.

reviewFlags.reason: This is a partial source chunk covering pages 1-26 of a 138-page report; later pages may contain additional test results and component tables.
Risk management files were not provided or evaluated for the component power supply; end-product risk management is required.
The power supply was not evaluated for direct patient connection, applied parts, SIP/SOP, power cord set, biocompatibility, and EMC at end-product level.
